annotations.label: Lie algebra

anchor.snippet: together with an operation called the Lie bracket

annotations.note: LieRing/LieAlgebra are defined exactly as a module with a bracket satisfying skew/Jacobi axioms.

annotations.label: Commutator Lie algebra from an associative algebra

anchor.snippet: every associative algebra gives rise to a Lie algebra

annotations.note: An associative algebra is given a Lie algebra instance via the ring commutator bracket.

annotations.label: Cross product Lie algebra on R^3

anchor.snippet: with Lie bracket defined by the cross product

annotations.note: `Cross.lieRing` makes `Fin 3 → R` a Lie ring under the cross product.

annotations.label: Fundamental example: linear maps of a vector space

anchor.snippet: the space of all linear maps from a vector space to itself

annotations.note: `Module.End R V` is a ring, hence a Lie algebra via the commutator instance.

annotations.label: Lie algebra (axioms)

anchor.snippet: satisfying the following axioms

annotations.note: The LieRing class bundles bilinearity, the alternating axiom `lie_self`, and the Leibniz/Jacobi identity.

annotations.label: Anticommutativity from bilinearity and alternating

anchor.snippet: bilinearity and the alternating property together imply

annotations.note: `lie_skew : -⁅y, x⁆ = ⁅x, y⁆` is derived from additivity and `lie_self`.

annotations.label: Anticommutativity implies alternating in char ≠ 2

anchor.snippet: then anticommutativity implies the alternating property

annotations.note: Mathlib takes `lie_self` (alternating) as the axiom and has no converse lemma recovering it from anticommutativity in characteristic ≠ 2.

annotations.label: Jacobi identity as Leibniz rule (derivation property)

anchor.snippet: allows to rewrite the Jacobi identity as a

annotations.note: The axiom `leibniz_lie` states the Jacobi identity in Leibniz/derivation form `⁅x,⁅y,z⁆⁆ = ⁅⁅x,y⁆,z⁆ + ⁅y,⁅x,z⁆⁆`.

annotations.label: Dimension of a Lie algebra

anchor.snippet: The dimension of a Lie algebra over a field means its dimension as a vector space

annotations.note: There is no Lie-specific dimension; one uses the underlying module's `Module.finrank`/`Module.rank`.

annotations.label: Generators of a Lie algebra

anchor.snippet: such that any Lie subalgebra (as defined below) that contains

annotations.note: `lieSpan` is the smallest Lie subalgebra containing a set, so a generating set is one whose `lieSpan` is `⊤`.

annotations.label: Abelian Lie algebra

anchor.snippet: A Lie algebra is called abelian if its Lie bracket is identically zero

annotations.note: `IsLieAbelian L` is defined as the bracket being trivial on `L`.

annotations.label: One-dimensional Lie algebras are abelian

anchor.snippet: Every one-dimensional Lie algebra is abelian

annotations.note: No named result asserting that a one-dimensional Lie algebra is abelian was found.

annotations.label: Commutator Lie bracket on an associative algebra

anchor.snippet: a Lie bracket may be defined by the commutator

annotations.note: The Lie ring on a ring has bracket `⁅x, y⁆ = x*y - y*x`.

annotations.label: Endomorphism Lie algebra gl(V)

anchor.snippet: The endomorphism ring of an

annotations.note: `Module.End R V` carries the commutator Lie algebra structure, equivalent to matrices via `lieEquivMatrix'`.

annotations.label: General linear Lie algebra gl_n(F)

anchor.snippet: the space of n × n matrices over F

annotations.note: `Matrix n n R` is a Lie algebra via the commutator and is Lie-equivalent to `Module.End R (n → R)`.

annotations.label: gl_n(R) as Lie algebra of GL(n,R)

anchor.snippet: is the Lie algebra of the general linear group

annotations.note: The Lie-group/Lie-algebra correspondence for GL(n,R) is not formalized.

annotations.label: Lie subalgebra

anchor.snippet: A Lie subalgebra is a linear subspace

annotations.note: `LieSubalgebra` is a submodule closed under the bracket.

annotations.label: Ideal of a Lie algebra

anchor.snippet: is a linear subspace that satisfies the stronger condition

annotations.note: `LieIdeal` is defined as a Lie submodule for the adjoint action.

annotations.label: Lie algebra homomorphism

anchor.snippet: A Lie algebra homomorphism is a linear map compatible with the respective Lie brackets

annotations.note: `LieHom` (`L →ₗ⁅R⁆ L'`) is a linear map preserving the bracket.

annotations.label: Isomorphism of Lie algebras

anchor.snippet: An isomorphism of Lie algebras is a bijective homomorphism.

annotations.note: `LieEquiv` (`L ≃ₗ⁅R⁆ L'`) is a bijective Lie homomorphism.

annotations.label: First isomorphism theorem for Lie algebras

anchor.snippet: The first isomorphism theorem holds for Lie algebras

annotations.note: `quotKerEquivRange : (L ⧸ f.ker) ≃ₗ⁅R⁆ f.range` is the first isomorphism theorem.

annotations.label: Commuting elements

anchor.snippet: are said to commute if their bracket vanishes

annotations.note: No dedicated predicate; commuting is just the proposition `⁅x, y⁆ = 0`.

annotations.label: Centralizer and center

anchor.snippet: The centralizer subalgebra of a subset

annotations.note: The center `LieAlgebra.center` is formalized, but a general centralizer subalgebra of an arbitrary subset is not.

annotations.label: Normalizer subalgebra

anchor.snippet: for a subspace S , the normalizer subalgebra of

annotations.note: `LieSubalgebra.normalizer` (and `LieSubmodule.normalizer`) implement the normalizer.

annotations.label: Diagonal matrices as abelian subalgebra

anchor.snippet: of diagonal matrices in

annotations.note: No named declaration for the diagonal matrices forming an abelian Lie subalgebra was found.

annotations.label: One-dimensional subspaces are abelian subalgebras

anchor.snippet: Every one-dimensional linear subspace of a Lie algebra

annotations.note: No named result that every one-dimensional subspace is an abelian subalgebra was found.

annotations.label: Product Lie algebra

anchor.snippet: the product Lie algebra is the vector space

annotations.note: `Prod.lean` provides `LieRing (L₁ × L₂)` and `LieAlgebra R (L₁ × L₂)` instances with componentwise bracket.

annotations.label: Semidirect product of Lie algebras

anchor.snippet: is said to be a semidirect product of

annotations.note: `SemiDirectSum K L ψ` (`K ⋊⁅ψ⁆ L`) is the semidirect sum twisted by a derivation map.

annotations.label: Derivation of an algebra

anchor.snippet: a derivation of A over F is a linear map

annotations.note: `Derivation R A A` is an R-linear map satisfying the Leibniz rule.

annotations.label: Derivations form a Lie algebra

anchor.snippet: is again a derivation

annotations.note: The commutator of two derivations is a derivation, giving `Derivation R A A` a Lie algebra structure.

annotations.label: Lie algebra of vector fields

anchor.snippet: the Lie algebra of vector fields

annotations.note: The Lie bracket of vector fields is defined, but not bundled into a `LieAlgebra` instance on the space of vector fields.

annotations.label: Inner derivation

anchor.snippet: The inner derivation associated to any

annotations.note: `LieDerivation.inner` sends `x` to the inner derivation `y ↦ ⁅x, y⁆`.

annotations.label: Outer derivations

anchor.snippet: the Lie algebra of outer derivations is defined as the quotient Lie algebra

annotations.note: The quotient of derivations by inner derivations is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Derivations of semisimple Lie algebras are inner

anchor.snippet: every derivation is inner

annotations.note: Every derivation of a finite-dimensional Killing (semisimple in char 0) Lie algebra is inner (`= ad x`).

annotations.label: Matrix group and its Lie algebra

anchor.snippet: A matrix group is a Lie group consisting of invertible matrices

annotations.note: Matrix Lie groups and the tangent-space construction of their Lie algebra are not formalized.

annotations.label: Recovering Lie group via matrix exponential

anchor.snippet: one can recover the Lie group as the subgroup generated by the matrix exponential

annotations.note: No result recovering a matrix Lie group from its Lie algebra via the matrix exponential was found.

annotations.label: Special linear group and sl_n

anchor.snippet: consists of all real n × n matrices with determinant 1

annotations.note: `sl` is defined as trace-zero matrices; the link to the group SL(n) is not formalized.

annotations.label: Orthogonal group and so_n

anchor.snippet: plays a basic role in geometry

annotations.note: `so` is defined as skew-adjoint matrices; the link to the group SO(n) is not formalized.

annotations.label: Complex orthogonal group and Lie algebra

anchor.snippet: are given by the same formulas applied to n x n complex matrices

annotations.note: `so` is defined over an arbitrary commutative ring, so it specializes to complex matrices.

annotations.label: Unitary group and u_n

anchor.snippet: that preserves the length of vectors in

annotations.note: The unitary Lie algebra of skew-Hermitian matrices is not defined (skew-adjoint there is for bilinear forms).

annotations.label: Special unitary group and su_n

anchor.snippet: is the subgroup of matrices with determinant 1 in

annotations.note: The special unitary Lie algebra su(n) is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Symplectic group and symplectic Lie algebra

anchor.snippet: that preserves the standard alternating bilinear form on

annotations.note: `sp` is the symplectic Lie algebra; the link to the symplectic group is not formalized.

annotations.label: Classical Lie algebras

anchor.snippet: The classical Lie algebras are those listed above

annotations.note: `Classical.lean` defines the type A/B/C/D Lie algebras `sl`, `so`, `sp`.

annotations.label: Unique nonabelian 2-dimensional Lie algebra

anchor.snippet: of dimension 2 over any field F , up to isomorphism

annotations.note: The classification of two-dimensional Lie algebras is not formalized.

annotations.label: Affine group as matrices

anchor.snippet: The affine group G can be identified with the group of matrices

annotations.note: The affine group and its associated Lie algebra are not formalized in this matrix form.

annotations.label: Heisenberg algebra

anchor.snippet: is the three-dimensional Lie algebra with a basis

annotations.note: No Heisenberg Lie algebra definition was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Heisenberg algebra is nilpotent

anchor.snippet: is nilpotent (though not abelian)

annotations.note: Since the Heisenberg algebra is not defined, its nilpotency is not formalized.

annotations.label: so(3) as skew-symmetric matrices

anchor.snippet: of the rotation group SO(3) is the space of skew-symmetric 3 x 3 matrices

annotations.note: Skew-adjoint matrices form a Lie subalgebra; so(3) is the n=3 case (also via `Orthogonal.so`).

annotations.label: so(3) is simple

anchor.snippet: it is simple , meaning that it is not abelian and its only ideals are 0 and all of

annotations.note: Simplicity of so(3) is not formalized.

annotations.label: sl(2) of trace-zero 2×2 matrices

anchor.snippet: Another simple Lie algebra of dimension 3

annotations.note: sl(2) is `sl (Fin 2) F`; `IsSl2Triple` also captures the sl₂ relations.

annotations.label: sl(2) is simple

anchor.snippet: is simple, and classify its finite-dimensional representations

annotations.note: Simplicity of sl(2) (and the classification of its representations) is not formalized as such.

annotations.label: sl(2,C) is the complexification of su(2)

anchor.snippet: is isomorphic to the complexification of

annotations.note: su(2) is not defined, so this isomorphism is not formalized.

annotations.label: Vector fields as infinite-dimensional Lie algebra

anchor.snippet: The Lie algebra of vector fields on a smooth manifold of positive dimension is an infinite-dimensional Lie algebra

annotations.note: The bracket of vector fields exists but is not packaged as a `LieAlgebra` instance.

annotations.label: Kac–Moody algebras

anchor.snippet: The Kac–Moody algebras are a large class of infinite-dimensional Lie algebras

annotations.note: The Serre construction of a Lie algebra from a Cartan matrix is formalized, but Kac–Moody algebras are not named/developed as such.

annotations.label: Moyal algebra

anchor.snippet: The Moyal algebra is an infinite-dimensional Lie algebra that contains all the classical Lie algebras as subalgebras.

annotations.note: The Moyal algebra is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Virasoro algebra

anchor.snippet: The Virasoro algebra is important in string theory

annotations.note: The Virasoro algebra is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Free Lie algebra

anchor.snippet: called the free Lie algebra on a vector space V

annotations.note: `FreeLieAlgebra R X` is defined with its universal property `FreeLieAlgebra.lift`.

annotations.label: Representation of a Lie algebra

anchor.snippet: on V is a Lie algebra homomorphism

annotations.note: Representations are modeled as `LieModule`s; `LieModule.toEnd : L →ₗ⁅R⁆ Module.End R M` is the corresponding homomorphism to gl(V).

annotations.label: Faithful representation

anchor.snippet: A representation is said to be faithful if its kernel is zero.

annotations.note: `LieModule.IsFaithful` states the action map `toEnd` is injective.

annotations.label: Ado's theorem

anchor.snippet: Ado's theorem states that every finite-dimensional Lie algebra over a field of characteristic zero has a faithful representation

annotations.note: Ado's theorem is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Adjoint representation

anchor.snippet: the adjoint representation is the representation

annotations.note: `LieAlgebra.ad : L →ₗ⁅R⁆ Module.End R L` with `ad x y = ⁅x, y⁆`.

annotations.label: Weyl's complete reducibility theorem

anchor.snippet: says that every finite-dimensional representation is a direct sum of irreducible representations

annotations.note: Weyl's complete reducibility theorem is not formalized.

annotations.label: Universal enveloping algebra

anchor.snippet: called the universal enveloping algebra

annotations.note: `UniversalEnvelopingAlgebra R L` is defined with its universal property `lift`.

annotations.label: Poincaré–Birkhoff–Witt theorem

anchor.snippet: It satisfies the Poincaré–Birkhoff–Witt theorem

annotations.note: Only the universal property is built; the PBW basis theorem is not formalized.

annotations.label: Classification of abelian Lie algebras

anchor.snippet: Every finite-dimensional abelian Lie algebra over a field

annotations.note: No classification result for abelian Lie algebras was found.

annotations.label: Commutator (derived) subalgebra

anchor.snippet: the commutator subalgebra (or derived subalgebra ) of a Lie algebra

annotations.note: The derived subalgebra `⁅L,L⁆` is `derivedSeries R L 1`, the first term of the derived series of ideals.

annotations.label: Nilpotent Lie algebra

anchor.snippet: is nilpotent if the lower central series

annotations.note: `IsNilpotent` is defined via vanishing of the `lowerCentralSeries` (nilpotency of L is `LieModule.IsNilpotent R L L`).

annotations.label: Engel's theorem

anchor.snippet: By Engel's theorem , a Lie algebra over any field is nilpotent if and only if

annotations.note: Engel's theorem is proved (for Noetherian Lie algebras over any commutative ring) via the `IsEngelian` framework.

annotations.label: Solvable Lie algebra

anchor.snippet: is said to be solvable if the derived series

annotations.note: `IsSolvable` holds iff some term of `derivedSeries` is `⊥`.

annotations.label: Radical of a Lie algebra

anchor.snippet: has a unique maximal solvable ideal, called its radical

annotations.note: `LieAlgebra.radical` is defined as the maximal solvable ideal.

annotations.label: Simple Lie algebra

anchor.snippet: is called simple if it is not abelian and the only ideals

annotations.note: `IsSimple` requires only ideals `⊥`/`⊤` and non-abelian.

annotations.label: Semisimple Lie algebra

anchor.snippet: is called semisimple if the only solvable ideal in

annotations.note: `IsSemisimple` (and the equivalent `HasTrivialRadical`) is defined; over char 0 it matches the zero-solvable-radical condition.

annotations.label: Semisimple iff product of simple Lie algebras

anchor.snippet: is semisimple if and only if it is isomorphic to a product of simple Lie algebras

annotations.note: Mathlib defines semisimple via independent simple atomic ideals summing to `⊤`, but the explicit `≅ product of simple` biconditional is not a single decl.

annotations.label: sl_n is simple

anchor.snippet: and every field F of characteristic zero (or just of characteristic not dividing n )

annotations.note: Simplicity of `sl n` is not formalized.

annotations.label: Semisimplicity of representations of semisimple Lie algebras

anchor.snippet: every finite-dimensional representation of a semisimple Lie algebra is semisimple

annotations.note: This (Weyl's theorem) is not formalized.

annotations.label: Reductive Lie algebra

anchor.snippet: is called reductive if its adjoint representation is semisimple

annotations.note: `HasCentralRadical` (radical = center) is the Mathlib notion of reductive in char 0, defined differently from the adjoint-rep-semisimple phrasing.

annotations.label: Structure of reductive Lie algebras

anchor.snippet: Every reductive Lie algebra is isomorphic to the product of an abelian Lie algebra and a semisimple Lie algebra.

annotations.note: The abelian × semisimple decomposition of reductive Lie algebras is not formalized.

annotations.label: Killing form

anchor.snippet: the Killing form , the symmetric bilinear form on

annotations.note: `killingForm R L` is the trace form of the adjoint representation.

annotations.label: Cartan's criterion for semisimplicity

anchor.snippet: is semisimple if and only if the Killing form is nondegenerate

annotations.note: Trivial radical ⟹ nondegenerate Killing form, and `IsKilling.instSemisimple` gives the converse.

annotations.label: Levi decomposition

anchor.snippet: The Levi decomposition asserts that every finite-dimensional Lie algebra over a field of characteristic zero is a semidirect product

annotations.note: The Levi decomposition is not formalized.

annotations.label: Killing–Cartan classification of simple Lie algebras

anchor.snippet: every simple Lie algebra is of type A n , B n , C n , D n , E 6 , E 7 , E 8 , F 4 , or G 2

annotations.note: The classification of simple Lie algebras by Dynkin type is not formalized.

annotations.label: Lie's third theorem

anchor.snippet: This is Lie's third theorem

annotations.note: Lie's third theorem (integration of Lie algebras to Lie groups) is not formalized.

annotations.label: Equivalence of categories for simply connected Lie groups

anchor.snippet: taking the Lie algebra gives an equivalence of categories from simply connected Lie groups to Lie algebras

annotations.note: This Lie group/Lie algebra equivalence of categories is not formalized.

annotations.label: Connected Lie group as universal cover mod discrete center

anchor.snippet: Every connected Lie group is isomorphic to its universal cover modulo a discrete central subgroup.

annotations.note: This Lie group structure theorem is not formalized.

annotations.label: Real form of a complex Lie algebra

anchor.snippet: is said to be a real form of

annotations.note: Base change/complexification `A ⊗[R] L` is formalized, but the notion of a real form is not defined.

annotations.label: Split form

anchor.snippet: a split form of it is a real form that splits

annotations.note: Split real forms are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Compact form

anchor.snippet: A compact form is a real form that is the Lie algebra of a compact Lie group.

annotations.note: Compact real forms are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Graded Lie algebra

anchor.snippet: a graded Lie algebra is a Lie algebra (or more generally a Lie superalgebra ) with a compatible grading

annotations.note: `GradedLieAlgebra ℒ` requires a bracket-compatible internal grading.

annotations.label: Differential graded Lie algebra

anchor.snippet: A differential graded Lie algebra also comes with a differential

annotations.note: Differential graded Lie algebras are not formalized.

annotations.label: Homotopy groups as a graded Lie algebra

anchor.snippet: the homotopy groups of a simply connected topological space form a graded Lie algebra

annotations.note: The Whitehead-product graded Lie algebra on homotopy groups is not formalized.

annotations.label: Lie algebra over a commutative ring (Lie ring)

anchor.snippet: over R is an R - module with an alternating R -bilinear map

annotations.note: `LieRing`/`LieAlgebra R L` are defined exactly over a general commutative ring R.

annotations.label: Lie ring from a group filtration

anchor.snippet: a construction of Lie rings arising from the study of abstract groups

annotations.note: The associated graded Lie ring of a group's lower central series is not formalized.

annotations.label: Lie ring of dihedral group of order 8

anchor.snippet: the Lie ring associated to the lower central series on the dihedral group of order 8 is the Heisenberg Lie algebra of dimension 3

annotations.note: This specific computation is not formalized.

annotations.label: Category-theoretic definition of Lie algebra

anchor.snippet: in the category of vector spaces together with a morphism

annotations.note: Mathlib defines Lie algebras via type classes, not as an internal object in the category of vector spaces.
